2603.20899.title: Mitigating Shortcut Reasoning in Language Models: A Gradient-Aware Training Approach
2603.20899.paper_summary: Large language models exhibit strong reasoning capabilities, yet often rely on shortcuts such as surface pattern matching and answer memorization rather than genuine logical inference. We propose Shortcut-Aware Reasoning Training (SART), a gradient-aware framework that detects and mitigates shortcut-promoting samples via ShortcutScore and gradient surgery. Our method identifies shortcut signals through gradient misalignment with validation objectives and answer-token concentration, and modifies training dynamics accordingly. Experiments on controlled reasoning benchmarks show that SART achieves +16.5% accuracy and +40.2% robustness over the strongest baseline, significantly improving generalization under distribution shifts. Code is available at: https://github.com/fuyanjie/short-cut-aware-data-centric-reasoning.
2603.20899.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型展現出強大的推理能力，但往往依賴於表面模式匹配和答案記憶等捷徑，而非真正的邏輯推理。我們提出了捷徑感知推理訓練（SART），這是一個梯度感知框架，通過捷徑分數和梯度手術來檢測和減輕促進捷徑的樣本。我們的方法通過與驗證目標的梯度不對齊和答案標記集中度來識別捷徑信號，並相應地修改訓練動態。在受控推理基準上的實驗顯示，SART 在最強基線上實現了 +16.5% 的準確率和 +40.2% 的穩健性，顯著改善了在分佈變化下的泛化能力。代碼可在此獲得：https://github.com/fuyanjie/short-cut-aware-data-centric-reasoning。
2603.20899.author: Hongyu Cao et.al.
2603.20899.authors: Hongyu Cao, Kunpeng Liu, Dongjie Wang, Yanjie Fu
